matches.match_rationale: This is the core capability for this pain point — predicting which individual students are likely to withdraw based on behavioral signals (attendance, fee arrears, academic performance, disciplinary incidents). Multi-site operators have exactly the structured per-student feature data this model requires. The output (withdrawal probability score per student) directly enables proactive outreach before families disengage.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A withdrawal-risk scoring model trained on historical enrollment data that assigns each enrolled student a weekly churn probability, surfaced in a retention dashboard for admissions staff to act on. Integrates with SIS/ERP to pull attendance, fee, and academic data automatically.

matches.match_rationale: Classifying students into risk tiers (e.g., low/medium/high/critical withdrawal risk) from structured SIS, fee, and attendance data is a direct application of tabular classification. Unlike regression propensity, this produces actionable buckets that map to specific intervention protocols — escalation to admissions team, pastoral outreach, finance review.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A multi-class risk classifier that segments the enrolled population into tiered risk categories each week, triggering workflow rules per tier (e.g., high-risk = automated flag to head of admissions within 24 hours).

matches.match_rationale: Sudden deviations from a student's normal behavioral baseline — abrupt attendance drop, fees going unpaid for the first time, dramatic grade decline — are anomalies that precede withdrawal. An anomaly detection layer catches these inflection points in real time rather than waiting for patterns to accumulate, enabling faster intervention than a propensity model trained on longer windows.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An early-warning anomaly engine that monitors student behavioral signals continuously and fires an alert when any individual's pattern deviates significantly from their own historical norm, feeding a daily exception report for retention coordinators.

matches.match_rationale: Once at-risk students are identified, the admissions team needs personalized outreach — emails, SMS scripts, and call guides tailored to the specific risk signals (e.g., a fee-arrears family requires a different tone than a family citing academic dissatisfaction). LLM-generated drafts grounded in the student's risk profile reduce staff effort and improve message relevance.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An outreach content generator that takes a student's risk profile (flags triggered, history summary) and produces a draft email or call script personalized to their situation, ready for staff review before sending.

matches.match_rationale: The end-to-end retention workflow — detect risk signal → retrieve student context → generate outreach → log interaction → schedule follow-up — involves multiple systems (SIS, CRM, email, finance). An automation agent orchestrates these steps without requiring manual handoffs across platforms, closing the gap between identification and action that currently causes families to slip through.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A retention workflow agent that, upon a risk threshold being crossed, automatically pulls student context, drafts a personalized outreach message, creates a task in the CRM for the assigned admissions officer, and schedules a follow-up reminder if no contact is logged within 48 hours.
